title: manager-prompt-placeholder-leak
description:
This is a continuation of manager-prompt-placeholder-leak. A high-severity prompt placeholder leak remains present across consecutive watcher intervals, and in this window it is reported in a manager_watcher prompt at the start of the interval. The rest of the factory activity in scope was operationally quiet: no failed runs, no retry cluster, no queue snapshot evidence of a stall, and no active tick-duration concern were recorded.

The current evidence indicates an ongoing prompt-assembly plumbing issue rather than a transient model failure. The affected persona run itself completed successfully at 2026-05-29T18:44:57.787456+00:00, so the immediate impact is prompt-quality and correctness risk rather than a hard execution failure. This matters because the leak has now persisted across at least two watcher-note intervals and has crossed personas, which suggests a factory-internal assembly path rather than a one-off story-domain artifact.

Impact in this window appears limited but real: the factory is still running, and no direct downstream breakage is visible in the bundled signals. A long dev run for story 27 also completed successfully, which argues against a broader runtime outage. The unchanged missing-DB worktrees are separate background drift and do not show new movement here.

Plausible explanation from the available evidence is a dispatch or prompt-construction bug that is allowing unresolved placeholders into manager-facing prompts. Because the detector explicitly labels any such record as high severity and the pattern has continued across intervals, the signal is clear enough to warrant L3 review even without a contemporaneous failed run.

evidence.summary_excerpt: A high-severity prompt placeholder leak remains present, now in a manager_watcher prompt at the start of this window.

evidence.excerpt: {"noteworthy":"A high-severity placeholder leak was recorded in a manager_watcher prompt, continuing the prompt-assembly issue seen in the prior interval."}

escalation_reason: Placeholder leakage in manager-facing prompts has persisted across consecutive watcher intervals and is now observed in manager_watcher, indicating an ongoing factory-internal prompt-assembly pattern rather than a one-off event. L3 judgment is needed because the issue is high-clarity, attributable to factory plumbing, and recurrence across intervals suggests it will continue until corrected.
